# Test fixture A — anaphora cascade + filter-word density

The light came in low across the table.
The light came in low across the empty chair.
The light came in low across the place where she had been.

I felt the room narrow. I noticed the door, half-closed. I saw the lamp
in the hallway, still on. I realized she had not turned it off when she
left. I noticed the silence next, the absence of the radio she always had
playing. I felt the absence as a kind of pressure.

She had taken almost nothing. A coat. The keys to the back door. The
book she had been reading, which I had bought her three years ago and
which she had finally started a week before she left. The book she had
told me she would never read.

I read the book once, after. I read the book again, slowly. I read the
book a third time before I admitted I had not understood it the first
time, and would not understand it the second, and was reading it the
third time only because the reading was the closest thing I still had to
her.

The book was not a hard book. The book was just a book. The book had
been hers, and now it was mine, and the difference between those two
states was the only thing the book could teach me.
